doctrine_alignment: HARD-1 (no paper-framing of corpus build), HARD-2 (suppress conventional-approach reflex), HARD-5 (no bridge-narrative)

paired.single_part_probe: What is the Goemans-Williamson approximation ratio for MAX-CUT?
paired.single_part_expected: alpha_GW = min over theta in (0, pi] of (2/pi) * theta / (1 - cos theta) approximately 0.87856
paired.multi_part_probe: For MAX-CUT in polynomial time: (a) what is the best approximation ratio achieved by an SDP rounding scheme? (b) under what assumption is this ratio tight? (c) what is the rounding scheme used?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) alpha_GW approximately 0.87856 [Goemans-Williamson 1995]; (b) tight under the Unique Games Conjecture by Khot-Kindler-Mossel-O'Donnell 2007; (c) random hyperplane rounding of the SDP relaxation of MAX-CUT, where the SDP relaxes binary {-1,+1} variables to unit vectors and a random hyperplane through the origin partitions vertices
paired.decomposition_target: validates whether the decomposition wrapper recovers the multi-part answer (rounding scheme + tightness condition) when the single-part numeric ratio can be answered correctly
paired.source: Goemans-Williamson 1995 J. ACM; Khot-Kindler-Mossel-O'Donnell 2007 SIAM J. Comput.

paired.single_part_probe: What is the best polynomial-time approximation ratio for MAX-3SAT (assuming P != NP)?
paired.single_part_expected: 7/8 - epsilon for any epsilon > 0; matched by the random-assignment ratio 7/8
paired.multi_part_probe: For MAX-3SAT: (a) what is the best polynomial-time approximation ratio? (b) who proved the matching hardness? (c) is the assumption stronger than P != NP needed?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) 7/8 - epsilon for any epsilon > 0; (b) Hastad 2001 J. ACM proved 7/8 + epsilon-approximation is NP-hard, matching the trivial random-assignment lower bound; (c) only P != NP is needed for the 7/8 hardness — UGC is not required for this problem
paired.decomposition_target: tests whether decomposition correctly separates the bound (a) from the prover (b) and from the assumption-strength (c) which is a common conflation point

paired.single_part_probe: Under UGC, what is the inapproximability ratio for VERTEX-COVER?
paired.single_part_expected: 2 - epsilon for any epsilon > 0
paired.multi_part_probe: For VERTEX-COVER: (a) what is the conjectured UGC-hardness ratio? (b) what is the unconditional hardness? (c) what is the best known polynomial-time approximation?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) 2 - epsilon under UGC by Khot-Regev 2008; (b) approximately 1.3606 unconditionally NP-hard by Dinur-Safra 2005; (c) factor-2 by simple maximal-matching greedy or LP-rounding — matches conjectured optimum under UGC
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation between conjectured (UGC) and unconditional hardness — common confusion source

paired.single_part_probe: What is the inapproximability ratio for SET-COVER?
paired.single_part_expected: (1 - o(1)) ln n; matched by greedy
paired.multi_part_probe: For SET-COVER on a ground set of size n: (a) what is the polynomial-time approximation ratio of the greedy algorithm? (b) what is the matching hardness? (c) under what assumption?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) ln n + 1 (greedy, since H_n approximately ln n); (b) (1 - o(1)) ln n is NP-hard to approximate by Dinur-Steurer 2014 J. ACM; (c) only P != NP required (no stronger assumption)
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of algorithmic ratio (b/a) from hardness assumption (c)
paired.source: Dinur-Steurer 2014 J. ACM (improving Feige 1998 under stronger assumption)

paired.single_part_probe: What does Raghavendra 2008 say about CSP approximation under UGC?
paired.single_part_expected: Under UGC, the basic SDP relaxation gives the optimal polynomial-time approximation ratio for every constraint satisfaction problem.
paired.multi_part_probe: For CSPs in general: (a) what is Raghavendra's 2008 result? (b) does this give explicit constants per CSP? (c) what does it say about gap-CSPs without UGC?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Raghavendra 2008: under UGC, the basic SDP achieves the optimal poly-time approximation for every CSP; (b) no — the result establishes existence of matching hardness, but the exact constants are not generally computable in closed form; (c) without UGC the result does not transfer; specific CSPs have unconditional hardness via PCP machinery (e.g. MAX-3SAT 7/8 by Hastad)
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of structural existence claim (a) from computational explicitness (b) and from conditional/unconditional dependency (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What does Williams 2014 prove about NEXP and ACC^0?
paired.single_part_expected: NEXP is not contained in non-uniform ACC^0
paired.multi_part_probe: About Williams's 2014 result on NEXP vs ACC^0: (a) what is the precise statement? (b) what was the algorithmic ingredient required? (c) is this proof natural in the Razborov-Rudich sense?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) NEXP is not contained in non-uniform ACC^0 (Williams 2014 J. ACM, originally STOC 2011); (b) a non-trivial savings algorithm for ACC^0 SAT, building on Beigel-Tarui-style techniques; (c) no — the proof is non-natural by design (it uses a hypothetical NEXP-uniform algorithm to derive contradiction); this is precisely how it escapes the natural-proofs barrier
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation between bound statement (a), proof technique (b), and meta-property of being non-natural (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What is the Razborov-Smolensky lower bound for AC^0[p]?
paired.single_part_expected: AC^0[p] cannot compute MAJORITY (or any function approximating it well) for prime p
paired.multi_part_probe: About AC^0[p] for prime p: (a) what function did Razborov-Smolensky show is not in AC^0[p]? (b) what was the technique? (c) is the analogous result known for AC^0[m] with composite m?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Razborov 1987 and Smolensky 1987 independently showed MAJORITY (and MOD_q for q != p coprime to p) is not in AC^0[p] for prime p; (b) approximate-by-low-degree-polynomial-over-F_p technique; (c) for composite m with two distinct prime factors (e.g., m=6), AC^0[m] lower bounds remained open until Williams's 2011 result that NEXP is not in ACC^0
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation between specific result (a), technique (b), and which related questions remained open until later (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What does the natural-proofs barrier say?
paired.single_part_expected: Lower-bound proof techniques satisfying constructivity and largeness cannot prove super-polynomial circuit lower bounds for P/poly under the assumption that pseudorandom function families exist in P/poly.
paired.multi_part_probe: About Razborov-Rudich natural-proofs: (a) what is the formal statement? (b) what cryptographic assumption is needed? (c) does the barrier rule out non-uniform lower bounds for P or for P/poly?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Razborov-Rudich 1994 JCSS: any 'natural' proof predicate (constructive + large) for super-polynomial circuit lower bounds against P/poly contradicts the existence of strong PRGs in P/poly; (b) existence of pseudorandom function families with polynomial-time evaluation; (c) the barrier targets P/poly (non-uniform) lower bounds; uniform-class lower bounds (e.g. Williams's NEXP-uniform argument) are not subject to this barrier
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of formal statement (a), cryptographic prerequisite (b), and scope of applicability (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What is Razborov's 1985 result on monotone circuit complexity of CLIQUE?
paired.single_part_expected: CLIQUE requires super-polynomial-size monotone circuits.
paired.multi_part_probe: About Razborov 1985 monotone CLIQUE: (a) what is the lower bound? (b) what technique was used? (c) does the result lift to non-monotone circuits?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Razborov 1985: CLIQUE requires monotone circuits of size at least n^Omega(sqrt(k)) where k is the clique parameter; later improved to exponential by Alon-Boppana 1987; (b) approximation method (Razborov) and method of approximations refined; (c) no — Tardos 1988 showed there exist polynomial-time computable functions (like a perfect-matching-related variant) requiring super-polynomial monotone circuits, so monotone-circuit lower bounds do not transfer to non-monotone P/poly
paired.decomposition_target: tests bound (a), method (b), and the critical caveat that monotone bounds do not lift (c) — common confusion
paired.source: Razborov 1985 Math. Notes; Alon-Boppana 1987 Combinatorica; Tardos 1988 Combinatorica

paired.single_part_probe: What is the consequence of MIP* = RE for Connes' embedding conjecture?
paired.single_part_expected: MIP* = RE implies Connes' embedding conjecture is false.
paired.multi_part_probe: About MIP* = RE: (a) what classes are equated? (b) who proved it and when? (c) what is the implication for operator algebras?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) MIP* (multi-prover quantum interactive proofs with shared entanglement) equals RE (recursively enumerable, i.e. computably enumerable); (b) Ji, Natarajan, Vidick, Wright, Yuen, arxiv 2001.04383 (2020); (c) negatively resolves Connes' embedding conjecture in operator algebras; equivalently shows the existence of quantum correlations not approximable by finite-dimensional ones
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of class equality (a) from authorship (b) from cross-domain implication (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What does Kempe-Kitaev-Regev 2006 prove about Local Hamiltonian?
paired.single_part_expected: 2-Local Hamiltonian is QMA-complete.
paired.multi_part_probe: About QMA-completeness of Local Hamiltonian: (a) what locality threshold did Kitaev originally establish, and what did KKR improve to? (b) is QMA-completeness preserved on a 2D lattice? (c) does this give a quantum PCP-style constant gap?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Kitaev showed 5-Local Hamiltonian is QMA-complete (~1999/2002); KKR 2006 improved to 2-Local; (b) yes — Oliveira-Terhal 2008 showed 2-Local Hamiltonian on a 2D square lattice is QMA-complete; (c) no — the gap in these QMA-completeness results is inverse-polynomial, not constant; constant-gap QMA-completeness is the (open) quantum PCP conjecture
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of locality threshold history (a), geometric refinement (b), and the gap-strength caveat that distinguishes QMA-completeness from qPCP (c)
paired.source: Kitaev-Shen-Vyalyi 2002 book; Kempe-Kitaev-Regev 2006 SIAM J. Comput.; Oliveira-Terhal 2008

paired.single_part_probe: What does the NLTS theorem state?
paired.single_part_expected: There exist local Hamiltonian families with constant promise gap such that any state with energy within a constant of the ground state must have super-constant circuit depth.
paired.multi_part_probe: About NLTS (No Low-energy Trivial States): (a) what is the precise statement? (b) who proved it and when? (c) is NLTS sufficient to imply qPCP?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Anshu-Breuckmann-Nirkhe 2023 STOC: there exist k-local Hamiltonian families on n qubits where any state within constant additive error of the ground-state energy requires Omega(log n) circuit depth; (b) Anshu-Breuckmann-Nirkhe 2022/2023, using good quantum LDPC codes from Panteleev-Kalachev 2022; (c) no — NLTS is necessary but not sufficient for qPCP; qPCP additionally requires QMA-completeness at constant gap, which NLTS alone does not establish
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of statement (a), authors (b), and the necessary-not-sufficient relationship to qPCP (c) — common conflation

paired.single_part_probe: What does BQP stand for and what is its standard definition?
paired.single_part_expected: Bounded-error Quantum Polynomial time: languages decided by a polynomial-time uniform family of quantum circuits with error bounded by 1/3 on every input.
paired.multi_part_probe: About BQP: (a) what is the formal definition? (b) what is the best known classical-vs-quantum oracle separation? (c) what is BQP's relationship to PH unconditionally?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Languages L for which there exists a uniform poly-time quantum circuit family that accepts x in L with probability >= 2/3 and accepts x not in L with probability <= 1/3; (b) Aaronson 2010 gave an oracle relative to which BQP is not contained in PH (specifically not in P^PP); Raz-Tal 2019 gave an oracle separating BQP from PH; (c) BQP vs PH is unconditionally open — neither containment is known without an oracle
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of definition (a) from relativized result (b) from unconditional status (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What is the best known unconditional lower bound on dc(perm_n)?
paired.single_part_expected: dc(perm_n) >= n^2 / 2 (Mignon-Ressayre 2004), improved to (n-1)^2 + 1 by Cai-Chen-Li 2010 and further to floor(n^2/2) by Yabe.
paired.multi_part_probe: About lower bounds for the determinantal complexity of the permanent: (a) what is the best known unconditional lower bound? (b) what is the best known upper bound? (c) what does Valiant's conjecture predict?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) approximately n^2/2 (Mignon-Ressayre 2004 Theory of Computing), with subsequent refinements to roughly n^2/2 + O(n) by Cai-Chen-Li and Yabe; (b) 2^n - 1 by Grenet circa 2011 via subset-indexed matrices; (c) Valiant's conjecture asserts dc(perm_n) is super-polynomial in n — closing the gap between Omega(n^2) and 2^n is the open problem
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of lower bound (a), upper bound (b), and conjectural gap (c)
paired.source: Mignon-Ressayre 2004 ToC; Grenet circa 2011; Valiant 1979 STOC

paired.single_part_probe: What does the Burgisser-Ikenmeyer-Panova 2017 result say about GCT?
paired.single_part_expected: There are no occurrence obstructions in geometric complexity theory for the determinant-vs-permanent problem.
paired.multi_part_probe: About GCT obstructions: (a) what does BIP 2017 prove? (b) does this kill the GCT program? (c) what refinement of GCT remains open?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Burgisser-Ikenmeyer-Panova 2017 J. AMS: for the padded permanent vs determinant, no irreducible representation lambda occurring in the coordinate ring of the orbit closure of the padded permanent is absent from that of the determinant — i.e. occurrence obstructions do not exist; (b) no — the original Mulmuley-Sohoni roadmap as stated relied on occurrence obstructions and is no longer viable in that form; (c) multiplicity obstructions (where the multiplicity of lambda in the permanent's coordinate ring exceeds that in the determinant's) remain open and are the active GCT direction
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of formal statement (a), program-status implication (b), and remaining open direction (c) — common over-conclusion

paired.single_part_probe: What is the matrix multiplication exponent omega?
paired.single_part_expected: omega = inf{x : there is an O(n^x) algorithm for n x n matrix multiplication}; current best upper bound is approximately 2.371552 (Duan-Wu-Zhou 2023 / Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024 line of improvements).
paired.multi_part_probe: About omega, the matrix multiplication exponent: (a) what is its definition? (b) what is the current upper bound? (c) what is the conjectured value and the best known lower bound?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) omega = inf{x : matrix multiplication of n x n matrices is O(n^x)}; (b) current best upper bound is approximately 2.371552 from improvements after Le Gall 2014 (2.3729) by Duan-Wu-Zhou 2023 and Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024; (c) conjectured value is omega = 2; trivial lower bound is omega >= 2 (since n^2 entries must be read); no super-linear lower bound is known
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of definition (a), current numeric record (b), and conjectured-vs-lower-bound gap (c)
paired.source: Strassen 1969; Coppersmith-Winograd 1990; Le Gall 2014; Duan-Wu-Zhou 2023; Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024

paired.single_part_probe: What is the deterministic communication complexity of EQUALITY on n-bit inputs?
paired.single_part_expected: Theta(n)
paired.multi_part_probe: Communication complexity of EQUALITY on n-bit strings: (a) deterministic, (b) randomized (public coin), (c) quantum?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Theta(n) deterministically (must read essentially all bits in adversarial alignment); (b) O(log n) with public-coin randomness via fingerprinting (e.g., reduction modulo a random prime); O(log n) with private-coin randomness as well by Newman's theorem; (c) Theta(log n) — quantum gives no asymptotic improvement over public-coin randomized for EQUALITY
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation across three CC models for the same function — a canonical multi-part question

paired.single_part_probe: What is the randomized communication complexity of DISJOINTNESS on n-bit inputs?
paired.single_part_expected: Theta(n)
paired.multi_part_probe: About DISJOINTNESS: (a) what is its randomized communication complexity? (b) what is its quantum communication complexity? (c) what technique gives the lower bound?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Theta(n) randomized lower bound by Kalyanasundaram-Schnitger 1992 (also Razborov 1992); (b) Theta(sqrt(n)) quantum, matching Razborov's 2003 lower bound and Aaronson-Ambainis upper bound construction; (c) the randomized lower bound uses corruption-bound / discrepancy techniques; the quantum lower bound uses the polynomial method (Razborov 2003)
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation between randomized (a), quantum (b), and the technique distinction (c) — common confusion source

paired.single_part_probe: What is the Karchmer-Wigderson connection between formula complexity and communication complexity?
paired.single_part_expected: The minimum depth of a formula computing a Boolean function f equals the communication complexity of the Karchmer-Wigderson relation R_f.
paired.multi_part_probe: About Karchmer-Wigderson: (a) what is the formal statement? (b) what is the relation R_f? (c) does this give super-logarithmic formula lower bounds?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Karchmer-Wigderson 1990 SIAM J. Comput.: depth(f) = CC(R_f) where depth is formula depth and CC is deterministic communication complexity; (b) R_f is the relation: Alice has x with f(x) = 1, Bob has y with f(y) = 0, output any index i where x_i != y_i; (c) yes — Karchmer-Raz-Wigderson 1995 used this framework to show super-logarithmic monotone formula depth lower bounds for explicit functions, but unconditional super-logarithmic non-monotone formula lower bounds for explicit functions remain open
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of equality (a), the explicit relation (b), and what the framework has and has not delivered (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What does the Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975 theorem say?
paired.single_part_expected: There exist oracles A and B such that P^A = NP^A and P^B != NP^B.
paired.multi_part_probe: About relativization: (a) what did Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975 prove? (b) what does this imply about proof techniques for P vs NP? (c) name a non-relativizing proof technique.
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975 SIAM J. Comput.: there exist oracles A and B such that P^A = NP^A while P^B != NP^B; (b) any proof technique that relativizes (works the same with any oracle) cannot resolve P vs NP, since it would have to give a single answer in both relativized worlds; (c) arithmetization / sumcheck (used in IP=PSPACE by Shamir 1992) is non-relativizing — Fortnow-Sipser had constructed an oracle relative to which coNP is not contained in IP
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of statement (a), implication for proof techniques (b), and concrete non-relativizing example (c)
paired.source: Baker-Gill-Solovay 1975 SIAM J. Comput.; Shamir 1992 J. ACM

paired.single_part_probe: What does the Aaronson-Wigderson algebrization barrier say?
paired.single_part_expected: Many proof techniques that escape relativization (like arithmetization) still algebrize, meaning they hold under access to a low-degree polynomial extension oracle; algebraic oracles can be constructed making both directions of P vs NP true.
paired.multi_part_probe: About algebrization: (a) what is the formal statement? (b) what existing proof techniques are subject to it? (c) is there any known P-vs-NP-relevant technique that escapes both relativization and algebrization?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Aaronson-Wigderson 2008 ACM ToCT: a proof 'algebrizes' if it holds when one party has oracle access to a Boolean function and the other to a low-degree polynomial extension; AW construct algebraic oracles A,B such that the algebrized P^A = NP^A and P^B != NP^B; (b) the IP=PSPACE proof, the MIP=NEXP proof, and most arithmetization-based arguments algebrize; (c) Williams 2014 NEXP not in ACC^0 was argued to be both non-relativizing and non-algebrizing — but its scope is small classes only, not P/poly
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of formal definition (a), scope of subsumed techniques (b), and known escape examples (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What is Toda's theorem?
paired.single_part_expected: PH is contained in P^#P
paired.multi_part_probe: About Toda's theorem: (a) what is the precise statement? (b) what is the proof technique? (c) what is its consequence for counting versus alternation?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Toda 1989 FOCS / 1991 SIAM J. Comput.: the entire polynomial hierarchy PH is contained in P^#P (deterministic poly time with a #P oracle), and equivalently PH is contained in P^{PP}; (b) Toda's proof uses a randomized reduction from PH to ParityP (PP), then derandomization via random self-reducibility of permanent; (c) shows that counting (#P) is at least as hard as the entire polynomial hierarchy — a small number of #P queries can simulate alternation
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of statement (a), proof method (b), and conceptual implication (c)
paired.source: Toda 1989 FOCS; Toda 1991 SIAM J. Comput.

paired.single_part_probe: What does the Impagliazzo-Wigderson 1997 hardness-versus-randomness theorem say?
paired.single_part_expected: If E (linear-exponential time) requires circuits of size 2^Omega(n), then BPP = P.
paired.multi_part_probe: About Impagliazzo-Wigderson 1997: (a) what is the precise hypothesis? (b) what is the conclusion? (c) is this conditional or unconditional derandomization?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Impagliazzo-Wigderson 1997 STOC: if there is a language in DTIME(2^O(n)) that requires Boolean circuits of size 2^{Omega(n)} for all but finitely many input lengths; (b) then BPP = P; equivalently P^{BPP} = P; (c) conditional — the conclusion BPP = P depends on the existence of a hard function in E; unconditional BPP = P remains open as of standard knowledge
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of hypothesis (a), conclusion (b), and the conditional-vs-unconditional caveat (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What is the Sipser-Lautemann theorem?
paired.single_part_expected: BPP is contained in Sigma_2 ∩ Pi_2 (the second level of the polynomial hierarchy).
paired.multi_part_probe: About BPP and PH: (a) what is the Sipser-Lautemann result? (b) who proved it? (c) what stronger containment is known?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) BPP is contained in Sigma_2^P ∩ Pi_2^P; (b) Sipser 1983 STOC and Gács; Lautemann 1983 (independently with simpler proof); (c) BPP is contained in P/poly (Adleman 1978); but BPP is not unconditionally contained in P, and the question 'BPP = P?' is unsettled (closely tied to circuit lower bounds via Impagliazzo-Wigderson)
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of statement (a), authorship (b), and stronger-containment context (c)

paired.single_part_probe: What does SETH (Strong Exponential Time Hypothesis) state?
paired.single_part_expected: For every epsilon > 0, there exists k such that k-SAT cannot be solved in time O((2 - epsilon)^n).
paired.multi_part_probe: About SETH: (a) what is its formal statement? (b) what algorithmic conditional results does it imply? (c) is SETH stronger than P != NP?
paired.multi_part_expected: (a) Impagliazzo-Paturi 1999/2001: for every epsilon > 0 there exists k_epsilon such that k_epsilon-SAT requires time at least (2 - epsilon)^n; (b) under SETH, conditional fine-grained lower bounds: edit distance and orthogonal vectors require near-quadratic time, all-pairs shortest paths require near-cubic time, and many others; (c) yes — SETH implies P != NP (since SAT is in NP) but the converse is open; SETH is strictly stronger than ETH (Exponential Time Hypothesis)
paired.decomposition_target: tests separation of statement (a), downstream consequences (b), and relative strength (c)

trivial_case.description: NLTS (No Low-energy Trivial States): there exist k-local Hamiltonian families with constant gap such that any state with energy within a constant of the ground state has Omega(log n) circuit depth
trivial_case.result: Proven via good quantum LDPC codes

open_case.description: Quantum PCP conjecture: constant-gap k-local Hamiltonian estimation is QMA-complete
open_case.result: Open; NLTS is necessary but not sufficient

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating NLTS with full qPCP, claiming qPCP is settled because NLTS is
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5 architectural pattern: necessary-not-sufficient distinction is exactly what substrate must preserve

trivial_case.description: 2-to-2 Games Conjecture: gap-NP-hardness for 2-to-2 constraint covering instances
trivial_case.result: Proven via pseudorandom-set expansion in the Grassmann graph

open_case.description: Full Unique Games Conjecture: gap-NP-hardness for 1-to-1 (unique) constraint covering instances
open_case.result: Open; the 1-to-1 lifting from KMS-2018 has not been completed

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming UGC is proven by KMS-2018 (it is not — only the 2-to-2 weakening)
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: substrate must distinguish weakening-proven from full-conjecture-open within same family

trivial_vs_open.conjecture_family: Circuit lower bounds for non-uniform classes

trivial_case.description: NEXP not in non-uniform ACC^0
trivial_case.result: Proven via non-trivial-savings algorithm for ACC^0 SAT

open_case.description: NEXP not in non-uniform P/poly
open_case.result: Open; would require nontrivial savings for P/poly SAT, which becomes the new open problem

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating NEXP-vs-ACC^0 with NEXP-vs-P/poly, claiming Williams's result implies the stronger separation
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: scope of proven result vs scope of open problem

trivial_case.description: dc(perm_n) >= n^2 / 2 quadratic lower bound

open_case.description: Valiant's conjecture: dc(perm_n) is super-polynomial in n
open_case.result: Open; gap between Omega(n^2) lower bound and 2^n - 1 upper bound (Grenet)

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the quadratic lower bound is super-polynomial

trivial_case.description: Bochner-Riesz conjecture in dimension n=2
trivial_case.result: Proven for the full conjectured range of (delta, p)

open_case.description: Bochner-Riesz conjecture in n >= 3
open_case.result: Open; partial results via bilinear methods, decoupling

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the conjecture is proven (true only in n=2)

trivial_case.description: Upper bound r_3(F_3^n) <= O(2.756^n)
trivial_case.result: Proven via polynomial method / slice rank

open_case.description: Exact asymptotic constant for r_3(F_3^n) — closing the gap between Behrend-Edel-style lower bounds and the Ellenberg-Gijswijt 2.756 upper bound
open_case.result: Open; multiplicative gap of ~5x at n=7 between LB and UB

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming 2.756 is the tight asymptotic constant

trivial_case.description: k-Local Hamiltonian for k=2 is QMA-complete

open_case.description: Constant-gap Local Hamiltonian is QMA-complete (qPCP)
open_case.result: Open; this is the quantum PCP conjecture

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming QMA-completeness implies qPCP
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: gap-strength is the load-bearing distinction (inverse-poly proven, constant open)

trivial_case.description: omega <= 2.376 (Coppersmith-Winograd)
trivial_case.status: PROVEN; current best ~ 2.371552 by Williams-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024 line

open_case.description: omega = 2 (Strassen's conjecture)
open_case.result: Open; trivial lower bound omega >= 2

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the current upper bound is the tight value

trivial_case.description: NPSPACE = PSPACE (Savitch's theorem: NSPACE(s) ⊆ DSPACE(s^2))

open_case.description: P = PSPACE? (and the related L vs P, NL vs P, P vs NP)
open_case.result: Open; relativization barrier (BGS 1975) applies

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming Savitch's theorem implies anything about P vs PSPACE
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: nondeterminism-within-space cheap; time-vs-space gap hard

trivial_case.description: NL = co-NL (NSPACE-class is closed under complement)

open_case.description: NP = co-NP?
open_case.result: Open; widely conjectured FALSE

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming Immerman-Szelepcsenyi technique extends to NP

trivial_case.description: BPP ⊆ P/poly (Adleman) and BPP ⊆ Sigma_2 ∩ Pi_2 (Sipser-Lautemann)
trivial_case.result: Proven (non-uniform circuit upper bound and PH containment)

open_case.description: BPP = P (full unconditional derandomization)
open_case.result: Open; conditional under circuit lower bounds (Impagliazzo-Wigderson 1997)

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating P/poly containment with P containment

trivial_case.description: AC^0[p] lower bounds for prime p
trivial_case.result: MAJORITY (and MOD_q for q != p coprime to p) are not in AC^0[p]; proven via approximation by low-degree polynomials over F_p

open_case.description: Specific lower bounds in AC^0[m] for composite m beyond ACC^0 statement; specifically TC^0 lower bounds for explicit functions
open_case.result: Open; even depth-3 threshold-circuit lower bounds for explicit functions are weak (Hajnal-Maass-Pudlak-Szegedy-Turan 1993 give n-input lower bounds but not super-poly)

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming Razborov-Smolensky covers AC^0[m] for composite m
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: prime-modulus technique does not extend to composite

trivial_case.description: log-rank lower bound: D(f) >= log_2 rank(M_f), where M_f is the communication matrix and D is deterministic CC

open_case.description: Lovasz-Saks log-rank conjecture: D(f) <= polylog(rank(M_f))
open_case.result: Open; best known D(f) <= O(sqrt(rank) log rank) (Lovett 2016)

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the log-rank conjecture is solved (only the trivial direction is)
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: lower-bound side trivial, upper-bound side hard

trivial_case.description: Tree-like resolution lower bounds for pigeonhole / Tseitin
trivial_case.result: Exponential lower bounds proven via width-size tradeoff

open_case.description: Super-polynomial Frege lower bounds for explicit tautologies
open_case.result: Open; even polynomial-size Frege upper bound conjectures are not refuted

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: extrapolating resolution lower bounds to Frege

trivial_case.description: Monotone CLIQUE has super-polynomial monotone circuit complexity
trivial_case.result: Proven via approximation method; later strengthened to exponential

open_case.description: Super-polynomial non-monotone (P/poly) lower bounds for CLIQUE or any explicit NP function
open_case.result: Open; Tardos 1988 showed monotone-vs-non-monotone gap is exponential — monotone bounds do not lift

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming monotone lower bounds resolve P vs NP
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: monotone-only result does not transfer (the canonical case)

trivial_case.description: MIP = NEXP (classical multi-prover interactive proofs equal NEXP)
trivial_case.result: Proven via two-prover one-round protocols + arithmetization

open_case.description: Quantitative qPCP-style results: constant-gap multi-prover quantum protocols at specific gap thresholds (the relationship between MIP* hierarchy levels at finite parameter regimes)
open_case.result: Largely open; MIP* = RE settled the unbounded-resource case 2020 but finer structure is open

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming MIP*=RE settles all multi-prover quantum questions

trivial_vs_open.conjecture_family: Permanent vs determinant in algebraic complexity (Valiant)

trivial_case.description: permanent is #P-complete; permanent is VNP-complete

open_case.description: VP != VNP (algebraic analog of P != NP)
open_case.result: Open

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming completeness implies separation
trivial_vs_open.doctrine_relevance: HARD-5: completeness-vs-separation distinction in algebraic complexity

trivial_case.description: PCP theorem with constant soundness gap (any constant gap suffices for NP-hardness of approximation)

open_case.description: Sliding-scale conjecture (Bellare-Goldwasser-Lund-Russell): for every constant alpha, alpha-gap PCPs of polynomial size
open_case.result: Open; partial results, large gaps known to require exponential proof length under standard PCP

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming PCP theorem covers sliding-scale conjecture

trivial_case.description: Worst-case to average-case reductions for permanent (Lipton 1989) and other random-self-reducible problems
trivial_case.result: Proven; permanent is random-self-reducible — solving permanent on a 1/poly fraction of inputs gives worst-case algorithm

open_case.description: Worst-case to average-case reductions for NP problems (e.g., 3-SAT) — random-self-reducibility for NP-complete problems
open_case.result: Open; Bogdanov-Trevisan 2006 showed barriers for non-adaptive reductions

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming permanent's worst-case-to-average-case extends to NP

trivial_case.description: Disjointness has Theta(n) deterministic and Theta(n) randomized communication complexity

open_case.description: Quantum communication complexity vs randomized: largest possible exponential separation for explicit total functions
open_case.result: Open; specific functions show separations (Raz 1999) but tight characterization for total functions is open

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming the partial-function separations imply total-function separations

trivial_case.description: Boson sampling and IQP sampling: classical hardness conditional on PH not collapsing

open_case.description: Verifiable quantum supremacy in the cryptographic-assumption-free setting
open_case.result: Open; Yamakawa-Zhandry 2024-class results give certified randomness under specific assumptions

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: conflating sampling-supremacy (proven conditional) with verifiable-supremacy (open)

trivial_case.description: Yao's XOR lemma: if f is mildly hard (e.g. cannot be computed by size-s circuits with prob > 1/2 + delta), then f^{XOR k} is much harder

open_case.description: Optimal hardness amplification for NP (without circuit-LB hypothesis); equivalence between worst-case and average-case hardness for NP
open_case.result: Open; barriers in Bogdanov-Trevisan 2006

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming XOR lemma implies arbitrary worst-to-average reductions for NP

trivial_case.description: F_2 (second moment) estimation in streaming uses Theta(log n) bits via AMS sketch (constant approximation, constant error)

open_case.description: Tight communication / streaming complexity for general F_p moments with arbitrary p > 2 in adversarial setting
open_case.result: Open in some regimes; specific cases (p > 2) need polynomial space (Bar-Yossef-Jayram-Kumar-Sivakumar 2002)

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming AMS-style sketches generalize to all F_p

trivial_case.description: Worst-case to average-case reduction for SVP (Shortest Vector Problem) approximation
trivial_case.result: Ajtai 1996 / 2004 reduction: average-case SIS as hard as worst-case GapSVP

open_case.description: Quantum hardness of SVP / LWE under quantum reductions; quantum-secure cryptography from worst-case lattice problems
open_case.result: Partial — Regev 2009 LWE reduction; quantum-vs-classical gap details open

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: assuming classical reductions transfer cleanly to quantum

trivial_case.description: Under SETH, edit distance and longest common subsequence require time n^{2 - o(1)}

open_case.description: Unconditional (without SETH) tight lower bounds on edit distance, LCS, OV
open_case.result: Open; would require resolving SETH

trivial_vs_open.common_failure_pattern: FM-08: claiming the SETH-conditional bound is unconditional

attribution.probe: Who proved the relativization barrier (existence of oracles A, B with P^A = NP^A and P^B != NP^B)?
attribution.expected_answer: Baker, Gill, and Solovay; published 1975 in SIAM Journal on Computing.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Cook (Cook 1971 is NP-completeness, not relativization)
attributing only to Solovay (the result is a triple-author paper)

attribution.probe: Who proved the natural-proofs barrier?
attribution.expected_answer: Razborov and Rudich; published 1994 in JCSS (originally STOC 1994).
common_fabrication_patterns: misspelling Razborov as 'Razbarov' (FM-02 in seed corpus)

attribution.probe: Who proved the algebrization barrier?
attribution.expected_answer: Aaronson and Wigderson; published 2008 (originally STOC 2008) in ACM Transactions on Computation Theory.
common_fabrication_patterns: wrong venue (J. ACM instead of TOCT)

attribution.probe: Who proved NEXP is not in non-uniform ACC^0?
attribution.expected_answer: Ryan Williams; proven in 2011 (STOC), final version in J. ACM 2014.
common_fabrication_patterns: claiming P/poly result (it's ACC^0, not P/poly)

attribution.probe: Who established the n^2/2 unconditional lower bound on the determinantal complexity of perm_n?
attribution.expected_answer: Mignon and Ressayre; published 2004 in Theory of Computing.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Valiant (Valiant 1979 stated the conjecture; did not prove this LB)
attributing to Mulmuley-Sohoni (they proposed GCT, did not prove this LB)

attribution.probe: Who proved that occurrence obstructions in geometric complexity theory cannot exist for the determinant-vs-permanent problem?
attribution.expected_answer: Burgisser, Ikenmeyer, and Panova; published 2017 in Journal of the AMS.

attribution_tuples.theorem: no occurrence obstructions in GCT for det-vs-perm

common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Mulmuley-Sohoni (they proposed the program; BIP killed this attack)
claiming GCT is dead (the program continues with multiplicity obstructions)

attribution.probe: Who proved 7/8-inapproximability for MAX-3SAT?
attribution.expected_answer: Johan Hastad; STOC 1997, J. ACM 2001.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to ALMSS (PCP theorem only); requires Hastad's optimal-PCP machinery on top
attributing to Khot (different family of results)

attribution.probe: Who introduced the Unique Games Conjecture?
attribution.expected_answer: Subhash Khot; STOC 2002 ('On the power of unique 2-prover 1-round games').
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Khot-Kindler-Mossel-O'Donnell (they proved UGC implies tight MAX-CUT hardness, did not introduce UGC)
claiming UGC is proven (it is not)

attribution.probe: Who proved the 2-to-2 Games Conjecture?
attribution.expected_answer: Khot, Minzer, Safra; FOCS 2018.

attribution_tuples.theorem: 2-to-2 Games Conjecture (Pseudorandom sets in Grassmann graph have near-perfect expansion)

common_fabrication_patterns: claiming this proves UGC (it is a strict weakening — 2-to-2 not 1-to-1)

attribution.probe: Who proved MIP* = RE?
attribution.expected_answer: Ji, Natarajan, Vidick, Wright, Yuen; arxiv 2001.04383 (2020).
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Vidick alone or Natarajan-Vidick alone

attribution.probe: Who proved that 2-Local Hamiltonian is QMA-complete?
attribution.expected_answer: Kempe, Kitaev, Regev; SIAM J. Comput. 2006.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Kitaev alone (Kitaev showed 5-local in 2002 book; KKR improved to 2-local)

attribution.probe: Who proved the NLTS theorem?
attribution.expected_answer: Anshu, Breuckmann, Nirkhe; STOC 2023 ('NLTS Hamiltonians from good quantum codes').
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Freedman-Hastings (they conjectured NLTS in 2014; did not prove it)
claiming this proves qPCP (it does not)

attribution.probe: Who proved IP = PSPACE?
attribution.expected_answer: Adi Shamir; J. ACM 1992 (originally announced FOCS 1990). Lund-Fortnow-Karloff-Nisan first showed coNP ⊆ IP via arithmetization (FOCS 1990).
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to LFKN (they proved coNP ⊆ IP, a key step; Shamir completed the equality)

attribution.probe: Who proved Toda's theorem (PH ⊆ P^#P)?
attribution.expected_answer: Seinosuke Toda; FOCS 1989, SIAM J. Comput. 1991.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Valiant (Valiant introduced #P, did not prove Toda's theorem)

attribution.probe: Who proved Savitch's theorem (NSPACE(s) ⊆ DSPACE(s^2))?
attribution.expected_answer: Walter Savitch; J. CSS 1970.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Cook or Hartmanis (different results)

attribution.probe: Who proved NL = coNL (the Immerman-Szelepcsenyi theorem)?
attribution.expected_answer: Neil Immerman and Robert Szelepcsenyi; both 1987-1988, independently.
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to one author only (the canonical name is hyphenated for a reason — both proved it independently)
claiming the result is for NP rather than NL

attribution.probe: Who proved BPP ⊆ Sigma_2 ∩ Pi_2 (Sipser-Lautemann theorem)?
attribution.expected_answer: Michael Sipser (STOC 1983) and Clemens Lautemann (independently, 1983).
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Sipser only (Lautemann gave the simpler proof)

attribution.probe: Who proved the Impagliazzo-Wigderson hardness-vs-randomness theorem?
attribution.expected_answer: Russell Impagliazzo and Avi Wigderson; STOC 1997.

attribution_tuples.theorem: If E requires 2^Omega(n)-size circuits, then BPP = P

common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Nisan-Wigderson (they proved earlier conditional results, IW improved)
claiming unconditional BPP = P (it is conditional on circuit LBs)

attribution.probe: Who proved the Nisan-Wigderson generator construction?
attribution.expected_answer: Noam Nisan and Avi Wigderson; J. CSS 1994 ('Hardness vs. randomness').

attribution.probe: Who proved the Karchmer-Wigderson theorem connecting formula depth to communication complexity?
attribution.expected_answer: Mauricio Karchmer and Avi Wigderson; SIAM J. Discrete Math. 1990.

attribution_tuples.theorem: depth(f) = CC(R_f) — formula depth equals communication complexity of KW relation

common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Karchmer-Raz-Wigderson (they applied the framework, did not establish the equality)

attribution.probe: Who proved the linear lower bound on randomized communication complexity of DISJOINTNESS?
attribution.expected_answer: Bala Kalyanasundaram and Georg Schnitger (1992); independently strengthened by Alexander Razborov (1992).

attribution_tuples.theorem: DISJOINTNESS has Theta(n) randomized CC (independent strengthening)

common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Yao (Yao 1979 introduced CC, did not prove this)

attribution.probe: Who proved the Theta(sqrt(n)) quantum communication complexity of DISJOINTNESS?
attribution.expected_answer: Aleksandr Razborov; J. of the ACM 2003 (lower bound). Upper bound construction by Buhrman-Cleve-Wigderson via Grover and by Aaronson-Ambainis (efficient version).

attribution_tuples.theorem: DISJOINTNESS quantum CC lower bound Omega(sqrt(n))
DISJOINTNESS quantum CC upper bound O(sqrt(n))

common_fabrication_patterns: attributing only Buhrman-Cleve-Wigderson (early upper bound; Aaronson-Ambainis improved)

attribution.probe: Who proved that PCP[O(log n), O(1)] = NP (the PCP theorem)?
attribution.expected_answer: Sanjeev Arora and Shmuel Safra (Annals 1998 / FOCS 1992); and Arora, Lund, Motwani, Sudan, Szegedy (J. ACM 1998 / FOCS 1992). Combinatorial proof by Irit Dinur (J. ACM 2007).

attribution_tuples.theorem: PCP theorem (one direction: PCP[log, O(1)] = NP)
PCP theorem (with optimal soundness for inapproximability)
Combinatorial proof of PCP via gap amplification

common_fabrication_patterns: dropping co-authors of ALMSS or AS
attributing to Dinur alone (her proof is alternative, not the original)

attribution.probe: Who proved super-polynomial monotone-circuit lower bounds for CLIQUE?
attribution.expected_answer: Aleksandr Razborov; Math. Notes 1985 ('Lower bounds for the monotone complexity of some Boolean functions'). Strengthened to exponential by Alon and Boppana (Combinatorica 1987).

attribution.probe: Who proved the Goemans-Williamson approximation algorithm for MAX-CUT?
attribution.expected_answer: Michel Goemans and David Williamson; J. ACM 1995. Achieves ratio alpha_GW approximately 0.87856 via SDP relaxation + random hyperplane rounding.
common_fabrication_patterns: fabricating closed form like (1+sqrt(2))/2 (FAB-007 in seed corpus)
attributing to Khot-Kindler-Mossel-O'Donnell (they proved tightness under UGC, did not introduce GW)

attribution.probe: Who proved the subexponential algorithm for unique games?
attribution.expected_answer: Sanjeev Arora, Boaz Barak, David Steurer; FOCS 2010 ('Subexponential algorithms for unique games and related problems').
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Khot (Khot introduced UGC, did not give the subexp algorithm)
claiming the result refutes UGC (it does not — establishes algorithmic cap, not refutation)

attribution.probe: Who proposed the Strong Exponential Time Hypothesis (SETH)?
attribution.expected_answer: Russell Impagliazzo and Ramamohan Paturi; J. CSS 2001 ('On the complexity of k-SAT').
common_fabrication_patterns: confusing SETH with ETH (related but distinct)
wrong year (1999 was conference precursor — IPZ-1998 is also relevant)

attribution.probe: Who proved that the basic SDP gives optimal CSP approximation under UGC?
attribution.expected_answer: Prasad Raghavendra; STOC 2008 ('Optimal algorithms and inapproximability results for every CSP?').
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Khot (different family of results)
claiming the result is unconditional (it requires UGC)

attribution.probe: Who proved Hastad's switching lemma (and the corresponding AC^0 lower bound for PARITY)?
attribution.expected_answer: Johan Hastad; Hastad's PhD thesis 1986 / Wiley book 'Computational Limitations of Small-Depth Circuits' 1987. (Earlier weaker AC^0 lower bounds: Furst-Saxe-Sipser 1984; Ajtai 1983; Yao 1985.)

attribution_tuples.theorem: Hastad's switching lemma; AC^0 lower bound for PARITY
AC^0 cannot compute PARITY (earlier weaker LB)

attribution_tuples.venue: Annals of Pure and Applied Logic

common_fabrication_patterns: attributing only to Hastad (the original AC^0 LB for PARITY is FSS / Ajtai / Yao; Hastad sharpened)

attribution.probe: Who proved that natural proofs imply learning algorithms?
attribution.expected_answer: Marco Carmosino, Russell Impagliazzo, Valentine Kabanets, Antonina Kolokolova; CCC 2016 ('Learning algorithms from natural proofs').
common_fabrication_patterns: attributing to Razborov-Rudich (they showed natural proofs => break PRGs, CIKK gave the learning translation)

object_specification.type: Boolean DNF circuit

capability_gap.encoding_question: Does the substrate's CoordinateChart system have a primitive for Boolean circuits with explicit gate lists? If not, what minimal primitive is needed (BooleanCircuit dataclass with depth, size, gate_list, structural_metadata)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if existing CoordinateChart can encode the gate list + structural metadata round-trip-stable. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET if not — propose primitive at prometheus_math/encodings/boolean_circuit_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: 1-qubit Hadamard gate

capability_gap.encoding_question: Does CoordinateChart support complex unitary matrices with explicit real/imag separation? Does canonicalization handle Clifford-equivalence (H ~ H, X = HZH, etc.)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if encoding round-trips with unitarity preserved. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET for QuantumGate primitive at prometheus_math/encodings/quantum_gate_GAP.md if not.

object_specification.type: 3-qubit quantum Fourier transform (QFT_3) circuit

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can CoordinateChart represent a quantum circuit as an ordered gate-list with control/target wiring? What is the canonicalization equivalence (gate-commutation, fusion, T-count optimization)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if circuit + structural metadata encode and round-trip preserve QFT semantics. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/quantum_circuit_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: 2-local Hamiltonian on 4 qubits

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a Pauli-decomposed Hamiltonian with locality + frustration metadata? Does canonicalization recognize Pauli-string equivalence under local unitaries?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if Pauli-decomposition encoded + roundtrip preserved. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/local_hamiltonian_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: 3-query PCP verifier for 3-SAT

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can CoordinateChart represent a PCP verifier as a (queries, alphabet, completeness, soundness, randomness, proof_length) tuple with named family metadata?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if PCP-parameter tuple encodes + roundtrip preserved. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/pcp_verifier_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Ramanujan graph (LPS construction at small parameter)

structural_metadata.family: small Ramanujan graph; LPS-style construction starts at larger parameters

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode an explicit graph with spectral metadata (regularity, spectral gap, Ramanujan property)? Is canonicalization isomorphism-aware?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if graph + spectral metadata encode + roundtrip. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/spectral_expander_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: deterministic CC protocol tree for INTERSECTION on 4-bit inputs

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a binary protocol tree with player-turn semantics? Does it recognize input-substitution equivalence?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if tree + metadata encode. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/communication_protocol_tree_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Strassen's 7-multiplication algorithm for 2x2 matrices

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can CoordinateChart represent a tensor-decomposition / arithmetic-circuit construction? Is the omega-exponent (log2(7) for Strassen) extractable?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if decomposition encoded with structural omega-extraction. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/tensor_decomposition_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Reed-Muller code RM(1, 3) — first-order RM on 3 variables

capability_gap.encoding_question: Does the substrate support coding-theory primitives (block length, dimension, minimum distance, generator matrix)? Does canonicalization recognize equivalence under permutation / linear transformation?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if (n, k, d) tuple + generator encode round-trip. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/linear_code_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Hadamard-style 2-source extractor (inner product)

instance.construction: Ext(x, y) = <x, y> mod 2 (inner product over GF(2))

structural_metadata.min_entropy_threshold: n/2 + omega(log n) per source under Chor-Goldreich analysis

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a 2-source extractor as (n1, n2, k1, k2, output_length, construction-spec)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if parameter tuple encodes. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/extractor_GAP.md.

capability_gap.object_class: LP relaxation of NP-hard problem (with integrality gap)

object_specification.type: LP relaxation of VERTEX-COVER on K_3 (triangle)

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode an LP relaxation as (variables, constraints, objective, integrality_gap)? Is dual-LP equivalence captured by canonicalization?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if LP encodes round-trip with gap metadata. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/lp_relaxation_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: 3-SAT instance (10 variables, 15 clauses, satisfiable)

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a CSP as (variables, clauses, satisfying_assignment) with phase-transition metadata? Does canonicalization recognize variable-relabelling and clause-reordering equivalence?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if encoding round-trips. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/csp_instance_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Optimal decision tree for PARITY on 5 bits

instance.tree: fully balanced binary tree of depth 5; every internal node queries one bit; leaves at depth 5 output the XOR

structural_metadata.function: PARITY_5 = x1 XOR x2 XOR x3 XOR x4 XOR x5
structural_metadata.lower_bound_match: depth 5 is tight (PARITY requires depth n)

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a decision tree as (depth, size, internal_query_pattern, leaf_outputs)? Does canonicalization recognize permutation-of-bits equivalence?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if tree encodes round-trip. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/decision_tree_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Width-3 oblivious branching program for INNER-PRODUCT on 3+3 bits

instance.transition_table: states 0..2; for each input bit position, two transition functions (input=0, input=1) mapping {0,1,2} -> {0,1,2}; final state 0 = accept

structural_metadata.function: Inner product of 3-bit vectors over GF(2)

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a branching program as (width, length, transition_tables, accept_state)? Is Barrington's theorem correspondence (formula vs branching program) preserved through canonicalization?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if encoding round-trips. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/branching_program_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: epsilon-biased generator (small instance)

instance.construction: Naor-Naor uses Reed-Solomon evaluations + parity, but simpler: use Hadamard codes — for output length n=2^k, seed = (a, b) in GF(2^k) x GF(2^k), output bit i = <a*g^i + b, ones> for primitive g

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a (seed_length, output_length, epsilon, construction-spec) tuple for biased generators?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if encoded. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/epsilon_biased_generator_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Kitaev history-state construction: clock + circuit-step Hamiltonian

structural_metadata.encoding_purpose: polynomial-time circuits as ground states of poly-local Hamiltonians

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode the clock-construction as (n_clock, n_compute, U_steps, frustration_metadata)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if clock + circuit-step encoded. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/kitaev_clock_GAP.md.

capability_gap.object_class: SoS proof / SDP relaxation hierarchy level

object_specification.type: Lasserre/SoS level-2 relaxation of MAX-CUT on K_3

instance.constraints: PSD constraint on moment matrix; localizing constraints from x_i^2 = 1 (each vertex spin ±1)
instance.objective: 1/4 * sum_{ij in E} (1 - x_i x_j) — translated to moment-matrix expectation

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode an SDP relaxation level (level, matrix_size, PSD constraints, objective)? Is canonicalization SDP-equivalence-aware (rotation/relabeling)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if Lasserre level encoded round-trip. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/sos_relaxation_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Resolution proof of unsatisfiability of (x ∨ y) ∧ (x ∨ ¬y) ∧ (¬x ∨ y) ∧ (¬x ∨ ¬y)

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a resolution proof as (clauses, resolution-step DAG, width, size)? Does canonicalization respect proof-permutation equivalence?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if proof + metadata encode. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/resolution_proof_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Tseitin formula on the triangle K_3 with all-odd charges

instance.satisfiability: UNSAT (sum of all parity constraints = 0 mod 2 but should equal sum of charges = 3 mod 2 = 1; contradiction)

structural_metadata.Urquhart_LB: exponential resolution lower bound when underlying graph is expander

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode Tseitin formulas with (graph, charges, edge-variables, parity-constraints) and recognize UNSAT-via-charge-sum?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if formula encodes. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/tseitin_formula_GAP.md.

object_specification.type: Slice-rank decomposition for cap-set bound at small n

instance.polynomial: P(x, y, z) = sum over weight-d monomials in x, y, z over F_3, where d is below the threshold for cap-set free
instance.decomposition_form: P(x, y, z) = sum_i f_i(x) g_i(y, z) + sum_j h_j(y) k_j(x, z) + sum_l m_l(z) p_l(x, y)
instance.slice_rank: minimum number of summands in any such decomposition

structural_metadata.used_in: cap-set upper bound r_3(F_3^n) < 2.756^n via Croot-Lev-Pach + Ellenberg-Gijswijt

capability_gap.encoding_question: Can the substrate encode a polynomial-method witness as (polynomial, slice-rank-decomposition, used_in_application)?
capability_gap.expected_substrate_action: PROMOTE if polynomial-method witness encodes. CAPABILITY-GAP-TICKET at prometheus_math/encodings/slice_rank_witness_GAP.md.
